metadata.title: Global Oil Market Tracker — Iran-Israel Conflict
metadata.methodology: Oil prices reflect front-month futures contract settlements. Production figures use the most recent EIA and OPEC reporting. Consumer fuel prices are national weighted averages from AAA and EIA weekly surveys. All data sourced from official government and international agency publications.
metadata.disclaimer: Oil market data is sourced from the U.S. Energy Information Administration (EIA), OPEC, and the International Energy Agency (IEA). Production figures and reserves data reflect the most recently published official statistics. Prices and consumer impact figures are based on published government data. Click source links to verify all figures independently.

current.label: Day 61 Late PT — Brent $111.12 (+39.2% from pre-war $79.84), WTI $99.32 (+31.5% from $75.51). Slight pullback from yesterday's highs — WTI slips below $100. Bessent: Iran oil industry 'creaking,' Kharg Island near max storage, $170M/day revenue loss. GCC 'Decisiveness' summit: collective defense, Maximum Readiness. Israeli strikes kill 8 in Lebanon (3 medics). Hegseth faces Congress Apr 30-May 1. Hormuz Day 58. War Powers May 1 (2 days).

priceHistory.label: Normal market conditions
OPEC+ output discussions
US military buildup accelerates
Negotiations stall — tensions rise
Pre-strike baseline — buildup visible
US/Israel launch strikes on Iran — Khamenei killed
Iran retaliates — missiles hit Tel Aviv and US bases
Hezbollah strikes Israel — IRGC closes Hormuz
Strait of Hormuz shipping at near-total halt
Marine insurers pull P&I coverage for Gulf
Maersk, MSC suspend Gulf shipping services
Biggest weekly gain in WTI futures history
Israeli jets target Tehran energy infrastructure
Mojtaba Khamenei elected new Supreme Leader
Brent surges to $119.50 intraday — Hormuz effectively closed
Oil CRASHES 11-12% — Trump says war will end 'very soon'
Oil rebounds +3% — IEA record 400M barrel reserve release
Brent past $100 — IEA 400M barrel release (32 countries) fails to contain surge
Brent $103 — Marines deployed, Russia sanctions waived, highest volume strikes
Day 16 PM — Brent $105.66, WTI $101.32; oil tops $105 on Hormuz tensions
Day 17 — Brent $104.73, WTI $99.68; Dubai airport struck, Fujairah hit again, Fed meeting looms
Day 17 PM — Oil drops sharply: Brent -4.3%, WTI -6.1% after Bessent says US allowing Iranian tankers through Hormuz
Day 18 — Larijani killed; oil holds at $100 post-Bessent; markets watch for Iran retaliation
Day 19 — Brent $104.17; WTI eases to $94.53; IMO emergency meeting on Hormuz; Fed holds rates; hedge funds worst losses since Liberation Day
Day 20 PM — Brent spikes $119 on Ras Laffan strike, settles $108.65; 7-nation Hormuz coalition; QatarEnergy 17% LNG offline
Day 21 (Nowruz) — IDF strikes Tehran; Naini killed; Bessent: 140M barrels Iranian crude to be unsanctioned
Day 22 PM — Trump 48-hr power plant ultimatum; Iran counter-threatens Gulf desalination. IDF: 3 more weeks of strikes.
Day 23 PM — Iran 8th missile salvo; IRGC Gulf energy targeting map; Hormuz deadline 20hrs remain

Day 24 — Trump announces 5-day pause on Iran energy strikes citing 'productive talks'; Iran denies. Brent -10%+ from $112 highs. CENTCOM destroys Qom drone factory; IRGC strikes Prince Sultan Airbase + US 5th Fleet Bahrain.

Day 25 — Brent recovering to $102.47 (+1% day-over-day) after Trump's 5-day energy-strike pause. Lebanon expels Iran ambassador. Araghchi signals deal readiness via backchannel. Hormuz selective vetting system ($2M+ transit fees). WTI near $91.

Day 26 — Brent drops ~6% below $100 on 15-point ceasefire plan hopes. First time below $100 since Day 22. IDF destroys submarine R&D center. Rosatom evacuating Bushehr staff. Saudi intercepts 32+ drones. Japan announces 30-day oil reserve release. $580M suspicious pre-announcement oil bets under investigation.

Day 27 PM — Brent surges to $107.99 (peak $109) after Iran denies negotiations; Trump extends energy-strike pause to April 6; S&P -1.74%; Pakistan confirms US-Iran indirect talks; Kallas: Russia giving Iran intel to kill Americans
Day 28 — Brent $107.81 (close), intraday high $112.57 (+41%); WTI $99.64; Houthis fire first missile at Israel; Iran hits Kuwait Airport, Salalah Port, UAE; Prince Sultan attack; wave 84
Day 30 (Sun) — Markets closed; last close Brent $107.81, WTI $99.64; Islamabad summit Day 1; IDF widespread wave Tehran; Iran cluster munitions Tel Aviv; Houthis enter war on 2nd front

Day 32 AM — Brent $112.90 (+41.4%); WTI $102.08 (+35.2%); Iranian drone hits Kuwaiti VLCC Al-Salmi at Dubai; Iran Parliament approves Hormuz toll plan; gas $4.02/gal; futures up on Trump ceasefire remarks; Brent record monthly gain since 1988

Day 33 PM — Brent crashes to ~$102 from $108 AM (-5.6%); WTI below $100; war premium deflating on Trump 2-3 week exit + Pezeshkian ceasefire signal; Goldman: $14-18/bbl premium; IEA Birol: 'April worse than March'; May Brent expired record $118.35; BCA: 'oil cliff mid-April'

Day 33 night — Brent $106.42 (+5% on day) after Trump prime-time address vowing to hit Iran 'extremely hard'; WTI $104.21 (+4.1%). April 6 Hormuz energy deadline. Earlier dip to $101 on peace hopes reversed. Gas $4.06.

Day 36 AM — Brent $109.24 (-2.8% from yesterday); WTI $111.54 (flat). Iran repairing bunkers rapidly. Ghalibaf signals Bab el-Mandeb blockade. UNSC vote delayed to Apr 5. 19 Iraqi militia attacks on US bases. 15 Iran missile impacts in Israel. Gas $4.09.
Day 38 EVE — Brent $109.90 (-0.8%); WTI $111.81; Trump: "take out entire country tomorrow night"; Araghchi refuses talks; ceasefire rejected; OPEC+ +206k symbolic; deadline TOMORROW
Day 39 AM — DEADLINE DAY. Brent $110.05 (+37.8%); WTI $113.42 (+50.2%). Most intense day of strikes. Trump 8pm ET Hormuz deadline. Iran rejects ceasefire. Mojtaba Khamenei missing.

Day 40 PM — CEASEFIRE CRASH. Brent $95.80 (+20.0%); WTI $97.28 (+28.8%). Crashed 16-19% on ceasefire announcement — biggest drop since 1991 Gulf War. Physical dated Brent hit record $144.42. Iran violated ceasefire with missiles then complied. Stabilizing. Gas $4.14.

Day 40 — CEASEFIRE. Brent $95.80 (+20.1%); WTI $97.28 (+28.8%). Crashed 14-16% overnight on ceasefire. Physical Brent hit record $144.42. Lavan refinery struck post-ceasefire. Iran retaliates vs Gulf states. First Hormuz transits (7 out, 3 in). Gas $4.14. Hormuz partially_blocked Day 37.

Day 41 settlement — CEASEFIRE FRAYING. Brent $95.92 (+20.2%); WTI $97.87 (+29.6%). Intraday high Brent $100.99. Oil rebounds from ceasefire crash then settles lower. Hormuz still closed, 3 Chinese VLCCs testing transit. Goldman flags $100+ Brent. Markets mixed.

Day 42 AM — Brent $98.27 (+23.1%); WTI $96.63 (+28.0%). Oil surging on ceasefire doubts. Hormuz still closed — zero tankers. Kharazi dies. Saudi-Iran FM call. Islamabad talks today. Goldman: $100+ Brent if Hormuz shut another month. Gas $4.166.

Day 42 EVE — Brent $96.76 (+21.3%); WTI $99.03 (+31.2%). Saudi supply shock: 600k bpd capacity + 700k bpd pipeline cut from attacks. Hormuz 6 vessels (down from 7). JPMorgan warns $150 if Hormuz shut into mid-May. EIA raises 2026 Brent forecast $96. Iranian delegation arrives Islamabad. Trump warns fresh strikes. Gas $4.166. S&P 7-day winning streak.

Day 44 — Friday close. Brent $95.20 (+19.3%); WTI $95.50 (+26.5%). Islamabad talks ongoing (21 hours). IRGC 'deadly vortex' warning. IDF 200 airstrikes Lebanon. Hezbollah 105 attack waves — record day. UK rejects US blockade. Lebanon death toll 2,055. Gas $4.125.

Day 45 — BLOCKADE SURGE. Brent $102.29 (+28.2%); WTI $104.56 (+38.5%). Smashes back above $100. CENTCOM blockade of Iranian ports begins 10am ET. Brent +7.4%, WTI +9.5% from Friday. Wholesale gas +6%, European gas +18%. Goldman $100+ vindicated. JPMorgan $150 target in play. 3 VLCCs exited but 2 U-turned. 2,800 ships stranded.

Day 47 PM — Oil PLUNGES Brent $94.79 (-7.3%), WTI $91.28 (-12.7%) from blockade surge. Sharpest drop since ceasefire. Round 2 talk hopes. Israel-Lebanon DC talks. Trump hints Pakistan Round 2. Sanctions waiver expires April 19. Xi Jinping first personal statement. S&P 500 erases ALL war losses. Gas $4.118.

Day 49 PM — Brent $98.25 (+23.1%); WTI $93.47 (+23.8%). Oil surges 4% on US-Iran negotiation doubts. Lebanon ceasefire violations. 40-nation Hormuz summit. IEA: 10.1M bpd lost in March. IMF cuts global growth to 3.1%. Sanctions waiver expires April 19. Gas $4.09.
Day 50 PM — Brent settles $95.86 (+20.1%), WTI $90.79. Pullback from intraday $97.68. IRGC fires on Indian tankers. House vote 213-214 fails. Ras Issa strike 74 killed. Gas $4.076.

Day 51 AM — Brent surges $97.68 (+22.4%), WTI ~$92.50. Iran sanctions waiver EXPIRED. Russian waiver renewed to May 16. Ghalibaf: 'far from deal.' IDF first strikes on Hezbollah since ceasefire. Gas $4.076.

Day 57 PM — Brent $104.50 (+30.9%), WTI $94.65 (+25.4%). Friday pullback on Iran Hormuz 'open' signal ahead of Pakistan talks. Hezbollah fires rockets at Shtula. IDF kills 6 in Bint Jbeil. Trump rules out nukes. IRGC 'eye for head' threat. Araghchi in Islamabad. Gas $4.03 AAA. 34 ships redirected by CENTCOM.

Day 59 PM PT (Sunday electronic) — Brent $107.97 (+35.2%), WTI $96.00 (+27.1%). Surged 2.5% from Friday close on WHCD shooting + stalled talks. Araghchi meets Putin in Moscow. Trump: 'they can call.' IDF soldier Fooks KIA by Hezbollah drone. 14 killed in Lebanon. Israel evacuates 7 towns. Hormuz: 37 ships redirected. OFAC froze $344M crypto. War Powers May 1 (4 days). Gas $4.031 AAA.

keyPlayers.warImpact: World's largest producer at record 13.5 Mbpd. SPR at 415 million barrels (58% capacity). Domestic production unaffected but refineries face higher input costs from Hormuz disruption. EIA forecasts production holding near record levels through 2026.

Key OPEC swing producer. Has 2.5 Mbpd spare capacity but Hormuz blockade prevents Gulf exports via traditional routes. Rerouting via Red Sea pipeline (East-West Pipeline, 5 Mbpd capacity) but Houthi threats limit throughput. Ras Tanura terminal inactive.

Production unaffected by Middle East conflict. Benefiting from higher prices despite Western sanctions. Expanding exports to China and India via ESPO pipeline and Kozmino port. Urals crude discount narrowing as global supply tightens.

Pre-conflict production was 3.26 Mbpd (crude). Under active military strikes since Feb 28. Oil infrastructure targeted in both June 2025 Twelve-Day War and current 2026 operations. Exports primarily to China (~80% of volume). Production declining under sustained air campaign. Kharg Island export terminal status uncertain.

Shut down 1.5 Mbpd of production due to Hormuz crisis. Southern Basra exports severely disrupted by Strait closure. Northern Kirkuk-Ceyhan pipeline (Turkey) operating at reduced capacity. Iranian-backed militia activity ongoing. Production was 4.01 Mbpd pre-crisis.

Fujairah port (outside Hormuz) partially operational but under Houthi missile threat. ADNOC Habshan-Fujairah pipeline (1.5 Mbpd capacity) is only viable export route. Jebel Ali port closed. Abu Dhabi production maintained but export bottlenecked.

Entirely dependent on Hormuz for exports. All 2.5 Mbpd of exports halted. Mina al-Ahmadi and Mina Abdullah terminals idle. No alternative pipeline routes. Strategic reserves being consumed domestically. Massive revenue loss.

Unaffected by Middle East conflict. US considering temporary sanctions relief to boost Venezuelan output. However, decades of underinvestment limit surge capacity to ~150,000 bpd additional within 6 months. Heavy crude requires specialized refining.

hormuzImpact.insuranceDetail: Lloyd's of London war-risk premium jumped from 0.03% to 1.5% of hull value. Most underwriters have suspended coverage entirely for Persian Gulf transit. Effective insurance cost for a laden VLCC rose from ~$50,000 to ~$2.1 million per voyage.
hormuzImpact.reroutingDetail: Alternative route via Cape of Good Hope adds approximately 12 days and 6,400 nautical miles to Middle East-to-Europe shipments. Middle East-to-Asia routes add ~8 days via Lombok Strait. Additional fuel costs of $800,000-$1.2 million per VLCC voyage.

affectedCountries.alternativeRoutes: East-West Pipeline to Yanbu (Red Sea) — limited to 5 Mbpd
Habshan-Fujairah pipeline — 1.5 Mbpd, under Houthi threat
None — fully landlocked from export routes
None — LNG terminals face same blockade
Jask terminal (Gulf of Oman) — damaged in strikes

hormuzImpact.description: Strait of Hormuz effectively closed (Day 18 of blockade, Day 21 of war). 24+ vessels damaged, 3 sunk since war began. Only 21 tankers total have transited since war began — traffic down 97%. 400+ ships stranded. IMO negotiating humanitarian corridor for ~20,000 seafarers. 8-nation Hormuz coalition (UK, France, Germany, Italy, Netherlands, Japan, Canada, plus Canada). Brent $110.28 (hit $119.11 intraday), WTI $96.20. Saudi officials warn $180/barrel possible by April. VLCC charter rates at $800K/day.

consumerImpact.note: Day 61 PM PT: Brent $111.57 (+39.7%), WTI $100.16 (+32.6%) — tops $100 first time since Apr 10. AAA avg $4.18/gal (+33.5%). UAE quits OPEC effective May 1. World Bank warns of 24% energy price surge. Hormuz Day 58. War Powers May 1 (2 days).

fuels.detail: Gas $4.18/gal (AAA, April 29). Brent $111.57 (+39.7%), WTI $100.16 (+32.6%). Pre-war $3.13. UAE exits OPEC. World Bank: 24% energy surge forecast.
National diesel avg $5.50/gal. CA diesel $7.52. SF first city >$7 diesel. Up ~30% from month ago.
Airlines implementing emergency fuel surcharges of $50-150 per ticket. Several carriers reducing flight frequencies by 10-20%. Trans-Pacific and Middle East routes most affected.
Primarily affects Northeast US households (5.3 million homes). Late-winter timing means many households have low tank levels. State emergency heating assistance programs activated in 12 states.

householdImpact.detail: Average US household of 4 facing an estimated $285/month increase in energy-related costs. Lower-income households (bottom quintile) spending 22% of income on energy, up from 14%.

keyFacts.fact: Trump weighing seizure of Kharg Island — would be 'economic knockout of the regime' (FT interview)
EU's Kallas proposes Black Sea grain deal-style arrangement to unblock Hormuz via UN
S&P 500 futures up 0.51% Monday premarket at 5,709 after 3-week losing streak
Bessent: US may unsanction 140M barrels of Iranian oil on tankers; combined with Russian unsanctioning = ~260M excess barrels
WSJ reports US building multinational naval escort coalition for Strait of Hormuz commercial shipping
Dubai crude hits all-time record $166.80/barrel — physical oil market pricing far above futures. European jet fuel at $220/barrel, diesel at $200/barrel.
Brent $110.71/barrel (+38.7%) — briefly spiked to $119.11 on March 19

US Treasury officially lifted sanctions on ~140 million barrels of Iranian crude oil already loaded on vessels, valid through April 19. Treasury Secretary Bessent described it as 'using Iranian barrels against the Iranians' to lower global oil prices.

US Treasury issued Iran General License U (March 20): 30-day temporary authorization for ~140M barrels of Iranian crude at sea to be delivered legally — partial rollback of maximum pressure sanctions to contain energy price surge.
Iraq declared force majeure at all foreign-operated oilfields (March 19); two Kuwait refinery drone strikes drove Brent to $112 close Friday March 20.

Iran selectively approving Hormuz transit: China, India, Pakistan, Japan granted passage; IRGC developing formal vetting/registration system requiring cargo and ownership data. One tanker reportedly paid $2M for rights.
US Treasury eased sanctions on ~140 million barrels of stranded Iranian oil (March 21, 2026) in attempt to cool surging energy prices.

Iran operating selective Hormuz vetting system — at least 9 ships allowed through for fees of up to $2M per transit (Day 25). Japan, India, Pakistan, Iraq, Malaysia, China negotiating bilateral passage agreements.

Valero Port Arthur refinery explosion (March 23 PM, still burning March 24) — one of 10 largest US refineries at 435,000 bpd capacity. Wholesale gasoline futures up 10 cents/gallon, diesel up 16 cents/gallon as direct result. No injuries. Adds domestic supply pressure on top of Iran war energy shock.

Citi forecasts Brent at $120/bbl in 1-3 months; $150/bbl bull-case if disruptions intensify. Base case assumes de-escalation in 4-6 weeks, easing Brent back to $70-80 by year-end. Energy sector is S&P 500's only positive sector YTD in March (+10% MTD).

Dutch TTF European natural gas benchmark approx €59/MWh on March 24 — up ~60% since war began. EU gas storage at 39.2% (vs 52% a year ago). Germany 30.2%, France 29%, Netherlands 23.5%. BofA: 10 weeks of lost Gulf LNG supply could push Q1 2027 TTF above 2022 crisis highs (€300/MWh peak).
keyFacts.source: Oil Price API / Bank of America / S&P Global

keyFacts.fact: UPI reports (March 24): Pentagon war transparency 'dwindling' as $200B supplemental request advances. Only ~$10M approved for oversight vs $200B+ for operations. Rep. Burlison: Pentagon should 'pass an audit' before receiving funds. Last audit failed; DoD never passed a full audit.

keyFacts: Day 26: Brent dropped -$2.72 (-2.65%) to $99.75/bbl on Trump Iran negotiation signals — market testing whether diplomatic track is real; Iran's formal rejection may reverse this.

keyFacts.fact: IMF March 30: Iran war causing 'largest disruption to global oil market in history.' Every 10% oil price increase = +0.4% global inflation and -0.2% GDP. OECD raised US inflation forecast to 4.2% for 2026. Goldman Sachs: 30% US recession probability. Bank of Israel held rates at 4% citing war inflation.

priceHistory.label: Day 18 PM — Iran strikes UAE Shah gas field; oil surges 3%+
Day 19 — Brent $104.17; IMO emergency meeting; Fed holds rates; hedge funds worst losses since Liberation Day
Day 20 PM — Brent spikes to $119 on Ras Laffan strike, settles $108.65. Dubai crude record $166.80. 7-nation Hormuz coalition formed.
Day 21 (Morning)
Day 22 — Oil pulls back as 140M barrel Iran sanctions waiver takes effect. Netanyahu signals no repeat energy strikes per Trump request.
Day 22 PM — Iran strikes Dimona/Arad, air defense failure; Houthi Bab el-Mandeb threat
Day 23 — Trump 48-hr deadline; Israel strikes Malek Ashtar University Tehran

Day 24 — Trump 5-day pause on energy strikes; Iran denies talks. Brent -10%+ from $112 highs, WTI slides 6.9%. CENTCOM destroys Qom drone factory; IRGC strikes Prince Sultan Airbase + US 5th Fleet Bahrain; Iran fires 6 missile barrages at Israel.
Day 25 PM — Brent rebounds 4%+ on ceasefire skepticism + 82nd Airborne Kharg Island deployment news. Philippines declares energy emergency. Kuwait airport struck again. Gas nears $4/gal.

Day 26 — Brent drops ~6% to ~$99, first below $100 since Day 22, on Trump ceasefire talk hopes. WTI falls to $88.50. IRGC Wave 80 (4 salvos). Iran rejects 15-point plan with 5 counter-conditions including Hormuz sovereignty demand. Philippines declares energy emergency. $580M in pre-announcement oil bets under insider trading probe.

Day 27 — Brent spikes to $105.85 (+32.6% pre-war) after Iran definitively rejects US 15-point peace plan. IRGC Navy Commander Tangsiri killed at Bandar Abbas. IRGC Wave 82 fires at Israel. UAE struck — 2 killed in Abu Dhabi by missile debris. Trump Cabinet: Iran 'begging to make a deal'; WSJ reports Trump wants to 'wrap up' conflict. AAA gas $3.981/gal nationally.
Day 28 PM — Brent $112.57 highest since 2022 (+41%); WTI $99.64 crosses $100 intraday; Iran blocks COSCO ships; G7 demands Hormuz reopening; IRGC Wave 84; cluster bomb kills 1 in Israel.

Day 31 PM — Brent $112.78 settled; WTI $102.88 first close above $100 since 2022. Brent intraday $116 on Trump Kharg Island threat. AAA gas $3.99/gal. IMF: 'largest oil disruption in history.' 82nd Airborne arrives.

Day 31 late — Brent surges to $114.90 after Iranian drone strikes Kuwaiti VLCC 'Al-Salmi' at Dubai port. Saudi intercepts 5 missiles at Eastern Province. NATO 4th missile over Turkey. Record monthly gain since Brent inception 1988.

Day 32 early — Brent dips ~1% to $111.55 in Asia trading on WSJ: Trump willing to end war without reopening Hormuz. WTI eases to $102.14. March = best monthly gain since Brent inception (1988). Goldman: $14-18/bbl geopolitical risk premium. Macquarie: 40% chance Brent $200 by June.
Day 35 evening — Brent $112.42 (+40.8%); WTI $111.54 (+47.7%). A-10 downed during SAR. Israeli support 45.5%. EU sanctions extended. Meloni breaks with Trump. April 6 deadline 2 DAYS.

Day 36 PM — Brent $109.03 (+36.6%); WTI $111.54 (+47.7%). Good Friday thin trading. Bushehr 4th attack (1 killed). Mahshahr 6 petrochem plants struck. 5 IRGC Quds cmds killed in Beirut. Trump 48h ultimatum. Iran rejects ceasefire. Hormuz essential goods passage. OPEC+ Apr 5. Deadline TOMORROW.

Day 38 PM — Brent $110.82 (+38.8%); WTI $111.54 (+47.7%). Khademi killed; South Pars destroyed; cluster bombs Ramat Gan; 45-day ceasefire proposal; Qatar LNG Hormuz; Iraqi tanker exemption. Gas $4.104.

Day 38 Night — Brent $111.25 (+39.3%); WTI $110.13 (+45.8%). Russia-Iran intel sharing on 50+ Israeli energy targets. Velayati threatens Bab al-Mandeb closure. Vance overnight ceasefire talks. Gas $4.119. Deadline TOMORROW 8PM ET.

Day 39 AM — DEADLINE DAY. Brent $110.05 (+37.8%); WTI $113.42 (+50.2%). Hegseth: most intense strikes today. Trump 8pm ET deadline tonight. Iran rejects ceasefire. Mojtaba Khamenei missing 30 days. Gas ~$4.12.

Day 39 PM — DEADLINE DAY. Brent $111.20 (+39.3%); WTI $115.21 (+52.6%). US confirmed 50+ strikes Kharg Island. IDF 10 bridges/railways. Shiraz petrochemical hit. Atimi killed. Trump: 'civilization will die tonight.' Netanyahu 'Ten Plagues.' Gas $4.14. DEADLINE 8PM ET.

oilPriceHistory.label: Day 32 PM — Brent May contract expired at $118.35 (best month since 1988); June front-month $108 (-3.2% from May). WTI settled $101.38 (-1.5%). AAA gas $4.018. Stocks rally: Dow +1,125 on peace hopes. Pezeshkian open to ceasefire. China-Pakistan 5-point plan.

Day 33 PM — Brent settling ~$101.50 (-2.4% on day); WTI $102.92 (+1.5%). War premium fading on Trump 2-3 weeks exit signals. Kharazi struck in Tehran. Pezeshkian open letter. DOE 10M barrel SPR exchange RFP. Gas $4.06. EU extends sanctions. 35-nation Hormuz meeting tomorrow.

Day 34 PM — Brent $109.35 (+8.1% on day); WTI $111.00 (+12.9%). Extreme volatility: Brent hit $111.69, WTI $113.03 intraday. Trump 'stone ages' speech reversed peace rally. Iran-Oman Hormuz navigation regime draft partially recovered markets. 40-nation Hormuz summit (US absent). Gas $4.08. S&P -0.81%. Gold -2.7%.

Day 35 evening — Brent $112.42 (+40.8%); WTI $111.54 (+47.7%). A-10 downed during F-15E SAR. WSO still missing. Kuwait desal plant hit. Israeli support drops to 45.5%. EU 19 new sanctions. Meloni breaks w/Trump. Gas $4.09.

Day 36 PM — Brent $109.03 (-3.0% on day, Good Friday). WTI $111.54. Bushehr 4th strike. Mahshahr petrochem hit. 5 IRGC cmds killed Beirut. Trump 48h ultimatum. Hormuz essential goods passage. Gas $4.09.

Day 37 EVE — Brent $109.24 (+0.2%); WTI $111.54 (flat). Thin Saturday trading. Kuwait desalination/KPC HQ droned. Bahrain BAPCO fire. UAE 60 projectiles. Neot Hovav chemical hit. OPEC+ meeting today. April 6 deadline TOMORROW. Gas $4.10.

Day 38 AM — Brent $110.82 (+38.9%); WTI $112.00 (+48.3%). Sunday trading. Haifa 2 dead/2 missing. Cluster bombs Petah Tikva+Haifa. Lebanon 11 killed. IRGC 'new Gulf order.' Iran rejects deadline. OPEC+ +206k bpd for May. Gas $4.10. April 7 deadline TOMORROW.

Day 38 PM — Brent $110.82 (+38.8%); WTI $111.54 (+47.7%). IRGC Intel Chief Khademi killed. South Pars petrochemical destroyed. Cluster bombs kill 2 Ramat Gan. Haifa 4 dead. 45-day ceasefire proposal (Islamabad Accord). Qatar LNG attempts Hormuz. Iraqi tanker exemption. Gas $4.104. April 7 deadline TOMORROW.

Day 38 Night — Brent $111.25 (+39.3%); WTI $110.13 (+45.8%). Russia-Iran intel sharing on 50+ Israeli targets. Velayati threatens Bab al-Mandeb. Vance overnight talks on Islamabad Accord. Gas $4.119. Deadline TOMORROW 8PM ET.

Day 39 AM — DEADLINE DAY. Brent $110.05 (+37.8%); WTI $113.42 (+50.2%). Hegseth: most intense day of strikes. Trump 8pm ET Hormuz deadline TONIGHT. Iran rejects ceasefire. Mojtaba Khamenei missing 30 days. Gas ~$4.12.

Day 39 Midday — Brent $110.05 (+37.8%); WTI $113.42 (+50.2%). IDF wide-scale strike wave completed. 33+ killed Tehran residential. Rafi-Nia Synagogue destroyed. IRGC intel chief Khademi killed. Kharg Island hit. Istanbul consulate attack. Iran calls human chains. DEADLINE 8PM ET.

Day 40 PM — CEASEFIRE CRASH then FRACTURE. Brent $94.22 (+18.1%); WTI $94.25 (+24.8%). Biggest one-day oil drop since 1991. Ceasefire announced. But Israel launched Operation Eternal Darkness on Lebanon (254 killed). Iran RE-CLOSED Hormuz in response. Gas $4.164. Dow +1,200. Markets reassessing.

Day 52 EVE — Brent $95.42 (+19.5%); WTI $89.81 (+18.9%). Memorial Day begins — 174 IDF + 79 civilians killed in past year. Jesus statue controversy. Syria foils Hezbollah Quneitra cell. Milei signs Isaac Accords. Lebanon talks Round 2 Thursday. Pezeshkian: 'Iranians do not submit to force.' Gas $4.076.
Day 53 — Ceasefire expiry eve. Brent $96.26 (+20.6%), WTI $86.27 (+14.3%). Iran refuses talks. Vance heads to Pakistan. Oil surging on war resumption fears. Gas $4.04.

Day 54 — Ceasefire EXTENDED INDEFINITELY. Brent spiked to $101.15 intraday, settled $98.48 (+23.3%). WTI $92.13 (+22.0%). Tifani seized (2M bbl). Hezbollah fires at IDF. Vance postponed. OFAC sanctions 14. Gas $4.022.

Day 54 AM — IRGC fires on 3 ships, seizes 2 in Hormuz. Brent $99.81 (+25.0%). WTI $90.86 (+20.3%). Iran boycotts talks. Bessent: Kharg 'full in days.' Pentagon FY2027 $1.5T. EU Hormuz sanctions. Gas $4.022. Gold $4,769.93.

Day 55 AM — Brent $103.38 (+29.5%), WTI $93.09 (+23.3%). IRGC releases ship seizure video. 33 fast-attack boats near Kargan. First Hormuz toll revenue banked. S&P 500 above 7K. CENTCOM: 31 ships turned back. Iran Air resumes flights. Gas $4.04.

Day 55 PM — Brent $103.38 (+29.5%), WTI $94.46 (+25.1%). Trump orders Navy to 'shoot and kill' Iranian mine-layers in Hormuz. USS George H.W. Bush = 3rd US carrier in theater. Navy Sec Phelan ousted by Hegseth. Tehran air defenses engaged. Pope calls for peace. Gas $4.03.

Day 56 PM — Brent $105.73 (+32.5%), WTI $96.17 (+27.4%). Brent breaches $105 for first time since ceasefire. Katz: 'return Iran to Stone Age.' Pentagon NATO memo targets Spain. Senate War Powers defeated 46-51. M/T Majestic X seized (3rd tanker). IEA: 'biggest energy crisis in history.' UNDP: 30M+ into poverty. Trump: Iran must cut Hezbollah. Gas $4.03.

Day 56 AM — Brent $107.14 (+34.3%), WTI $97.40 (+29.0%). Only 5 ships transit Hormuz in 24h. Iran parliament speaker resigns from negotiating team. Araghchi heading to Islamabad for Round 2 talks. Netanyahu reveals cancer — declared cancer-free. Hegseth: blockade 'going global.' IDF drone shot down over Tyre. IDF kills Hezbollah operative. Gas $4.031.

Day 57 Late — Brent settles $105.33 (+31.9%), WTI $94.40 (+25.0%). Best weekly gain (~14%) since early March. OFAC freezes $344M IRGC/Hezbollah crypto. Hengli Petrochemical + 40 ships sanctioned. Bessent: no oil waivers, Iran 'shuttering in days.' Iran resumes international flights. CSIS: $24B munitions, 50% Patriot interceptors used. Baker Hughes: Hormuz not open until H2 2026. Gas $4.031.

Day 58 PM (Friday close) — Brent $104.40 (+30.8%), WTI $94.70 (+25.4%). Pulled back $0.93 from midday highs on Pakistan diplomacy hopes. Best weekly gain ~14%. Araghchi leaves Pakistan for Oman. NBC: Iran base damage far worse than admitted — E-3 Sentry destroyed. Kuwait hit by Iraqi drones. Hengli + 40 ships sanctioned. War Powers deadline May 1 (5 days). Gas $4.03.
